text:
Check out Sports IllustratedГўВҖВҷs Daily Rings, our daily Olympics podcast from Mitch Goldich and Dan Gartland. Subscribe wherever you get your podcasts (Apple,Spotify) and find clips onSIГўВҖВҷs YouTube page.

The U.S. womenГўВҖВҷs national basketball team does not often find itself in close games.ГӮ

Seeking an eighth consecutive gold medal, the squad arrived in Paris carrying an Olympic record of 72ГўВҖВ“3, with the most recent of those losses from before most of this roster was even born. (It came against the Soviet Unified Team in 1992.) The dynasty is among the most impressive examples of international sporting dominance. It has been possible to watch an entire Games without seeing the U.S. women so much as flirt with the mere idea of stress in the second half.ГӮ

But while close calls during international play are very, very rare for Team USA, theyГўВҖВҷre not unheard of. ThereГўВҖВҷs one that might feel particularly relevant now.ГӮ

The most recent country to seriously pressure the U.S.? Belgium. And next up in group play for the U.S.? Belgium.ГӮ

The country is not exactly known as an international basketball powerhouse. But at an Olympic qualifying game in February, the Americans needed a buzzer beater to defeat the Belgians, escaping with a final score of 81ГўВҖВ“79. It made for a surprising display of fallibility for the U.S. women. A fairly undersized Team Belgium was able to hold nearly even with Team USA both on the boards and in the paint while comfortably outscoring them from three. ItГўВҖВҷs fair to point out the Americans were not at full strength that day: They were missing star forward AГўВҖВҷja Wilson and point guard Chelsea Gray. Yet the situation was similar for the Belgians, who also missed two starters, including 6ГўВҖВҷ4ГўВҖВқ center Kyara Linskens. For their Olympic game on Thursday, the heavy favorite is still, of course, the Americans. But this rematch stood out on the schedule for both nations.ГӮ

For the U.S., the qualifying contest was a reminder that even for this strongest of teams, nothing is guaranteed. If this game felt like an important learning experienceГўВҖВ”itГўВҖВҷs rare for this group to get a test of their execution in the fourth quarterГўВҖВ”itГўВҖВҷs one they cannot afford to see again. And for Belgium, it was an encouraging sign that even against the stiffest of competition, this group can hold its own.ГӮ

ГўВҖВңIt kind of gave us confidence that we can compete on the highest level and that we really belong,ГўВҖВқ says point guardJulie Vanloo, the only Belgian woman playing this season in the WNBA. ГўВҖВңI think we made a little statement.ГўВҖВқ

That statement added to one the Belgians have steadily been making over the last few years. If this group was once an afterthought in the international basketball landscapeГўВҖВ”not so much anymore. The Belgian women qualified for their first Olympics in Tokyo and won their first EuroBasket title last June. Much of the team has stayed together in Europe this year to continue training as a group. (Vanloo is the only Belgian player in the W this season but is not the only one with experience in the league: The big name here is 2019 WNBA Finals MVP Emma Meesseman.) Yet the team opened group play with a surprising upset loss to GermanyГўВҖВ”which only increases the importance of this game against the U.S. A seriously competitive matchup is a long shot. But the Belgians will remind each other that was the case in February, too, and they still managed to bring it down to the wire there.

ГўВҖВңWe know Team USA is just the top of the top,ГўВҖВқ says Vanloo, who led Belgium in scoring in that February qualifying game with 19. ГўВҖВңThey have depth, they have a threat on every level, so we knew that. But the thing isГўВҖВ”we were O.K. with being underdogs. WeГўВҖВҷre O.K. being a country that plays without that pressure.ГўВҖВқ

The most serious threat Belgium posed in that qualifying game came from beyond the arc. Team Belgium shot 12-for-29 from three; Team USA went just 4-for-19. This would seem to be the recipe for testing the U.S. as much as possible: hope a player like Vanloo gets hot from three while doing everything possible to mitigate damage inside. Even that is likely to end in a loss. (The U.S. is simply that good.) But itГўВҖВҷs the most logical pathway towards a tight contest. And it surely has not been lost on Belgium that the U.S. struggled with the three-ball in its otherwisedominant opening win over Japan.ГӮ

The qualifying game from February is one the coaching staff has gone back to many times since, U.S. head coach Cheryl Reeve said.ГӮ

ГўВҖВңA game like that, in an environment like that, I think that will prove to be a valuable, valuable experience for us as we continue to move forward towards being in Paris,ГўВҖВқ Reeve said just before the team left for the Olympics.

Of course, Wilson being on the court for the U.S. this time should be a serious change. The consensus best player in the WNBAГўВҖВ”the consensus best player in the worldГўВҖВ”is a nightmare to face in the paint on both ends of the floor and more versatile than ever. But allow Vanloo the chance to make a few counterarguments. She points out that Belgium adding Linskens is not to be overlooked. ГўВҖВңSheГўВҖВҷs the most skilled post player IГўВҖВҷve ever played with,ГўВҖВқ Vanloo says. ГўВҖВңSheГўВҖВҷs very underrated.ГўВҖВқ And she adds what she views as another asset for her squad: home court advantage.

The group play for basketball is taking place in Lille, rather than in Paris, which puts the games close to the border with Belgium. (ItГўВҖВҷs less than an hour by train to the Basketball Belgium headquarters in Brussels.) The team is treating these as home games, essentially, and they plan to have a stadium full of supporters to match.

ГўВҖВңWeГўВҖВҷre so close to where our base is, where we always practice,ГўВҖВқ Vanloo says. ГўВҖВңFor us, itГўВҖВҷs easy to go home.ГўВҖВқ

The Americans, naturally, are still heavily favored to win, even before a sea of fans clad in black, yellow and red. But after their close call in February, this group knows not to take any chances.

ГўВҖВңItГўВҖВҷs going to be a great game,ГўВҖВқ says Team USA forward Napheesa Collier. ГўВҖВңTheyГўВҖВҷre a really good team. So I think itГўВҖВҷs going to be a battle.ГўВҖВқ

Emma Baccellieri is a staff writer who focuses on baseball and women's sports for Sports Illustrated. She previously wrote for Baseball Prospectus and Deadspin, and has appeared on BBC News, PBS NewsHour and MLB Network. Baccellieri has been honored with multiple awards from the Society of American Baseball Research, including the SABR Analytics Conference Research Award in historical analysis (2022), McFarland-SABR Baseball Research Award (2020) and SABR Analytics Conference Research Award in contemporary commentary (2018). A graduate from Duke University, sheГўВҖВҷs also a member of the Baseball Writers Association of America.

title: Why Belgium Is a Surprisingly Tricky Matchup for U.S. WomenГўВҖВҷs Basketball
description: After the Belgians brought it down to the wire in February, ThursdayГўВҖВҷs matchup stands out on the schedule for both nations.ГӮВ
